articles.headline: India Advocates for Equitable Global Climate Finance Reforms at UNGA
articles.explainer:
• India's External Affairs Minister addressed the UN General Assembly, emphasizing the need for equitable and accessible climate finance for developing nations.
• Highlighted the disproportionate burden faced by developing countries in combating climate change despite lower historical emissions.
• Proposed reforms to multilateral development banks (MDBs) to enhance their capacity for climate financing.
• Stressed the importance of technology transfer and capacity building alongside financial support.
• Called for developed nations to fulfill their $100 billion annual climate finance commitment.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora – their structure, mandate.
articles.source: Ministry of External Affairs / UN News
prelimsPoints: UN General Assembly (UNGA) is the main deliberative, policymaking and representative organ of the UN.
The $100 billion climate finance commitment was made by developed countries under the UNFCCC framework.
Multilateral Development Banks (MDBs) include institutions like the World Bank, IMF, ADB, AfDB, EBRD, IDB.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze India's stand on climate finance and its implications for global climate action.
Discuss the challenges faced by developing countries in accessing climate finance and propose solutions.
Examine the role of MDBs in addressing climate change and suggest reforms for enhanced effectiveness.
articles.imageDescription: Indian delegation addressing the UN General Assembly podium.
articles.trivia: The UN General Assembly convenes annually in regular session from September to December, and thereafter as required.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures, Focus on Growth
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its latest review, keeping the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• Citing signs of easing retail inflation and robust economic growth projections, the MPC adopted a cautious 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• Emphasis was placed on monitoring global commodity prices and geopolitical developments that could impact domestic inflation.
• Governor highlighted the resilience of the Indian economy and the need to support sustainable growth while managing price stability.
• Announced measures to enhance liquidity management and strengthen financial sector stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.
The primary objective of monetary policy in India is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks in the event of any shortfall of funds.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its likely impact on the Indian economy.
Discuss the challenges faced by the MPC in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global scenario.
Evaluate the effectiveness of RBI's monetary policy tools in achieving its stated objectives.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India headquarters building.
articles.trivia: The first Governor of the Reserve Bank of India was Sir Osborne Smith, while the first Indian Governor was C.D. Deshmukh.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Directs Centre to Mediate Inter-State Water Dispute, Upholds Federal Spirit
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a significant ruling, directed the Union government to actively mediate in a long-standing inter-state water dispute between two major states.
• Emphasized that while judicial intervention is necessary, resolution through cooperative federalism is paramount for such disputes.
• Highlighted the constitutional provisions empowering the Centre to facilitate amicable settlements in water-sharing conflicts.
• Urged states to engage in constructive dialogue, reminding them of their shared responsibility towards national development.
• The ruling aims to prevent prolonged litigation and promote collaborative governance in resource management.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: Article 262 of the Indian Constitution deals with adjudication of disputes relating to waters of inter-state rivers or river valleys.
The Parliament has enacted the Inter-State Water Disputes Act, 1956, and the River Boards Act, 1956.
Inter-State Water Disputes Tribunals are established under the 1956 Act.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of the Supreme Court and the Union government in resolving inter-state water disputes, citing relevant constitutional provisions.
Discuss the concept of cooperative federalism in the context of resource sharing in India and its challenges.
Suggest reforms to the existing dispute resolution mechanisms for inter-state water conflicts to ensure timely and equitable solutions.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: The first Inter-State Water Disputes Tribunal was established in 1969 to resolve the Krishna water dispute.

articles.headline: India and Maldives Conclude Joint Naval Exercise 'Ekatha' Enhancing Maritime Security
articles.explainer:
• India and Maldives successfully concluded the 17th edition of their joint naval exercise, 'Ekatha', in the Indian Ocean region.
• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability, maritime domain awareness, and HADR (Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief) operations.
• Participating units practiced anti-piracy drills, search and rescue operations, and communication procedures.
• The exercise underscores the commitment of both nations to regional security and stability in the strategically important Indian Ocean.
• High-level observers from both navies emphasized the importance of such engagements for mutual security interests.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
keyTerms: SAGAR (Security And Growth for All in the Region)
prelimsPoints: Exercise 'Ekatha' is a regular joint naval exercise between India and Maldives.
Maldives is a key maritime neighbour of India in the Indian Ocean.
India's 'SAGAR' doctrine emphasizes security and growth for all in the Indian Ocean Region.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of India-Maldives defense cooperation in the context of India's broader maritime security strategy.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in strengthening its security partnerships with Indian Ocean littoral states.
Evaluate the role of joint military exercises in enhancing regional stability and addressing common security threats.
articles.imageDescription: Naval ships of India and Maldives participating in a joint exercise.
articles.trivia: Maldives is the smallest Asian country in both land area and population.

articles.headline: India Unveils Blueprint for Global Digital Public Infrastructure Framework at G20 Side Event
articles.explainer:
• Building on its G20 presidency achievements, India presented a comprehensive framework for the development and adoption of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) globally.
• The blueprint, showcased at a G20 side event, draws heavily from India's successful 'India Stack' model (Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker).
• Aims to facilitate cross-country collaboration, knowledge sharing, and capacity building for nations seeking to implement DPI.
• Emphasized DPI's potential for financial inclusion, governance efficiency, and equitable access to services.
• Called for international cooperation to ensure open, secure, and inclusive digital ecosystems worldwide.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics & IT / G20 Secretariat
prelimsPoints: India Stack comprises Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker, and consent layer architecture.
DPI refers to shared digital systems that are foundational for public and private sector innovation.
G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
mainsPoints: Examine the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its transformative potential for economic development and governance.
Discuss how India's 'India Stack' model can serve as a blueprint for other developing nations, highlighting both successes and challenges.
Analyze the role of international cooperation in building secure, inclusive, and interoperable digital public infrastructures globally.
articles.imageDescription: Infographic showing components of Digital Public Infrastructure.
articles.trivia: UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in August 2023.

articles.headline: Government Notifies Stricter Environmental Impact Assessment Rules for Industrial Projects
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) notified revised Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) rules.
• The new regulations aim to streamline the clearance process while ensuring more stringent environmental safeguards for industrial and infrastructure projects.
• Key changes include enhanced public consultation mechanisms, clearer definitions for 'green' projects, and stricter post-clearance compliance monitoring.
• Introduced provisions for a national-level expert appraisal committee and state-level committees for faster, yet thorough, assessments.
• Addresses concerns raised by environmental activists regarding transparency and accountability in project approvals.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: EIA is a process of evaluating the likely environmental impacts of a proposed project or development, taking into account inter-related socio-economic, cultural and human-health impacts.
The first EIA notification in India was issued in 1994, under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
Public consultation is a crucial component of the EIA process.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the revised EIA rules in balancing economic development with environmental protection in India.
Discuss the key challenges in implementing effective EIA processes and suggest measures to enhance transparency and accountability.
Evaluate the role of public participation in environmental governance and its impact on project clearances.
articles.imageDescription: Factory emitting smoke, with a green leaf overlay representing environmental regulations.
articles.trivia: The Environment (Protection) Act, 1986, was enacted after the Bhopal Gas Tragedy.

articles.headline: ISRO Outlines Roadmap for Chandrayaan-4 Mission, Focus on Lunar Sample Return
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) released initial details and a roadmap for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission.
• The mission's primary objective is to achieve a lunar sample return, bringing back Moon rocks and soil for scientific analysis.
• Chandrayaan-4 is envisioned as a complex multi-module mission, potentially involving a lander, rover, and an ascent module.
• This mission builds upon the successes of Chandrayaan-2 and Chandrayaan-3, leveraging advanced indigenous technologies.
• Emphasizes India's growing capabilities in deep space exploration and its commitment to contributing to global lunar science.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Chandrayaan-3 successfully landed on the Moon's South Pole in August 2023.
ISRO's first lunar mission, Chandrayaan-1, was launched in 2008.
Lunar sample return missions are highly complex and have been achieved by only a few nations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the scientific significance of lunar sample return missions and how Chandrayaan-4 can advance our understanding of the Moon.
Analyze India's growing prowess in space exploration and its implications for global space partnerships and national development.
Examine the technological challenges involved in a lunar sample return mission and ISRO's strategies to overcome them.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of a lunar lander and rover collecting samples on the Moon.
articles.trivia: Only three countries (USA, Russia, China) have successfully brought back lunar samples to Earth so far.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Amendments to Digital Personal Data Protection Bill
articles.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) submitted its report on the Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023, recommending several key amendments.
• The recommendations focus on strengthening data fiduciary accountability, enhancing user consent mechanisms, and clarifying data localization requirements.
• Suggested a more robust framework for cross-border data transfers and stricter penalties for data breaches.
• Addressed concerns regarding government access to personal data and the independence of the Data Protection Board.
• The report paves the way for further debate and potential modifications before the Bill is presented for parliamentary approval.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
articles.source: Parliament of India / Lok Sabha Secretariat
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023, aims to provide for the processing of digital personal data in a manner that recognizes both the right of individuals to protect their personal data and the need to process such data for lawful purposes.
The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21.
Data Protection Board of India is proposed to be established under the Bill.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023, and the amendments proposed by the Parliamentary Committee.
Discuss the challenges in balancing individual privacy rights with national security and economic development imperatives in the digital age.
Evaluate the potential impact of the Bill on India's digital economy, technological innovation, and international data flows.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract representation of data flow and security.
articles.trivia: The first attempt at a comprehensive data protection law in India was the Srikrishna Committee Report in 2018.

articles.headline: India-Brazil Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Expanding Bilateral Trade and Investment
articles.explainer:
• India and Brazil held their annual Strategic Dialogue, focusing on boosting bilateral trade and investment across various sectors.
• Discussions included cooperation in agriculture, renewable energy, defense manufacturing, and pharmaceuticals.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to strengthening BRICS and IBSA frameworks for South-South cooperation.
• Explored avenues for reducing trade barriers and diversifying their trade basket to achieve a target of $20 billion by 2030.
• Emphasized shared values of democracy and multilateralism in addressing global challenges.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests.
keyTerms: BRICS (Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa)
IBSA (India, Brazil, South Africa Dialogue Forum)
prelimsPoints: Brazil is India's largest trading partner in the Latin American region.
BRICS is an association of five major emerging national economies.
IBSA Dialogue Forum is a trilateral developmental initiative.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-Brazil strategic partnership for both nations and the broader South-South cooperation agenda.
Discuss the potential for enhancing bilateral trade and investment between India and Brazil, identifying key sectors and challenges.
Evaluate the role of groupings like BRICS and IBSA in promoting multilateralism and addressing global economic imbalances.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Brazil together.
articles.trivia: Brazil is the only Portuguese-speaking country in South America.

articles.headline: New Conservation Initiative Launched for Ganges River Dolphin Protection
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, in collaboration with state governments, launched a new initiative for the conservation of the Ganges River Dolphin.
• The program focuses on habitat restoration, reducing pollution in river ecosystems, and community engagement to minimize human-dolphin conflicts.
• Includes enhanced anti-poaching measures and scientific research to monitor dolphin populations and health.
• Aims to protect the endangered species, which is India's National Aquatic Animal.
• Emphasizes the importance of healthy river ecosystems for both biodiversity and human well-being.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and Environment.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: The Ganges River Dolphin (Platanista gangetica) is listed as 'Endangered' on the IUCN Red List.
It is India's National Aquatic Animal.
It is found primarily in the Ganges-Brahmaputra-Meghna river system.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Ganges River Dolphin and the threats it faces in its natural habitat.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current conservation strategies and suggest comprehensive measures for its long-term survival.
Examine the interlinkages between river pollution, biodiversity conservation, and sustainable livelihoods for communities living along river banks.
articles.imageDescription: Ganges River Dolphin swimming in a river.
articles.trivia: Ganges River Dolphins are functionally blind and rely on echolocation to navigate and hunt.

articles.headline: Review Meeting Highlights Progress and Challenges in National Education Policy Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education held a high-level review meeting to assess the progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation.
• Key achievements highlighted include curriculum reforms, increased focus on vocational education, and digital learning initiatives.
• Challenges identified pertain to infrastructure development, teacher training, and equitable access in remote areas.
• States were urged to accelerate reforms, particularly in early childhood care and education (ECCE) and higher education accreditation.
• Emphasized the importance of continuous monitoring and adaptive strategies for successful NEP rollout.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
It aims to transform India's education system to meet the needs of the 21st century.
Key pillars include access, equity, quality, affordability, and accountability.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in reforming India's education system and fostering human resource development.
Discuss the major challenges in the implementation of NEP 2020, particularly in terms of infrastructure, teacher training, and digital divide.
Suggest concrete measures to ensure equitable and effective rollout of NEP across all states and educational levels.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a classroom with modern learning tools.
articles.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was instrumental in shaping India's education policy, recommending a common education structure.

articles.headline: India and Japan Hold Strategic Energy Dialogue, Focus on Green Hydrogen and Renewables
articles.explainer:
• India and Japan conducted their latest Strategic Energy Dialogue, reaffirming commitment to clean energy transition and energy security.
• Discussions centered on accelerating cooperation in green hydrogen production, renewable energy technologies, and energy efficiency.
• Explored joint ventures in critical mineral supply chains necessary for battery manufacturing and EV production.
• Emphasized the importance of diversifying energy sources and reducing reliance on fossil fuels for climate goals.
• Both nations aim to strengthen collaboration on smart grids and energy storage solutions.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests.
prelimsPoints: Japan is a major investor in India's infrastructure and energy sectors.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
India aims for 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of India-Japan energy cooperation in achieving energy security and climate goals for both nations.
Discuss the potential of green hydrogen and renewable energy technologies in transforming India's energy landscape.
Examine the challenges in developing a robust critical mineral supply chain and the role of international partnerships like India-Japan.
articles.imageDescription: Solar panels and a wind turbine against a blue sky, symbolizing renewable energy.
articles.trivia: Japan's bullet train technology has been adopted for India's Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail project.

articles.headline: Report Highlights Progress and Gaps in India's Health Sector Digitalization
articles.explainer:
• A recent report by a leading think tank assessed the progress of digitalization in India's health sector, particularly under the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
• Noted significant advancements in creating digital health IDs, electronic health records (EHRs), and teleconsultation services.
• Identified gaps including uneven adoption across states, infrastructure deficits in rural areas, and challenges in data interoperability and privacy.
• Recommended enhanced investment in digital health infrastructure, skilled workforce development, and robust data governance frameworks.
• Stressed the potential of digital health to improve accessibility, affordability, and quality of healthcare services.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) aims to develop the backbone necessary to support the integrated digital health infrastructure of the country.
It focuses on creating a seamless online platform through the provision of a wide range of data, information and infrastructure services.
The National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for ABDM.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the transformative potential of digitalization in improving healthcare access, efficiency, and quality in India.
Discuss the major challenges in implementing the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) and achieving universal digital health coverage.
Suggest policy measures to address issues of digital divide, data privacy, and interoperability in India's health sector digitalization efforts.
articles.imageDescription: Doctor using a tablet for patient consultation, symbolizing digital health.
articles.trivia: India's first National Health Policy was formulated in 1983.

articles.headline: Urban Local Bodies Adopt New Strategy for Enhanced Urban Flood Management
articles.explainer:
• Several major urban local bodies across India announced the adoption of a new comprehensive strategy for urban flood management.
• The strategy integrates early warning systems, improved drainage infrastructure, nature-based solutions, and community preparedness programs.
• Focuses on leveraging technology like AI and IoT for real-time monitoring of weather patterns and water levels.
• Aims to reduce urban vulnerability to extreme rainfall events and climate change impacts.
• Calls for inter-agency coordination and citizen participation for effective implementation.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including waterbodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs / NDMA
prelimsPoints: Urban flooding is caused by intense rainfall that overwhelms the capacity of drainage systems.
Nature-based solutions include green infrastructure like permeable pavements, rain gardens, and wetlands.
The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) issues guidelines for urban flood management.
mainsPoints: Analyze the major causes and consequences of urban flooding in India, linking it to climate change and unplanned urbanization.
Discuss the effectiveness of current urban flood management strategies and propose a holistic approach incorporating technology and nature-based solutions.
Examine the role of urban local bodies, state governments, and citizens in building climate-resilient cities against flood hazards.
articles.imageDescription: Flooded urban street with cars partially submerged.
articles.trivia: Mumbai's Mithi River, once a major natural drain, has been severely encroached upon, exacerbating urban flooding in the city.

articles.headline: India Reaffirms Commitment to Indus Waters Treaty Amidst Ongoing Discussions
articles.explainer:
• India's Ministry of External Affairs reaffirmed its commitment to the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) during ongoing technical discussions with Pakistan.
• The discussions, held under the Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) framework, addressed issues related to water sharing and project construction on western rivers.
• Emphasized India's desire for amicable resolution of differences through established mechanisms of the Treaty.
• Highlighted India's rights to utilize the waters of the western rivers for non-consumptive uses as per the IWT.
• The Treaty, brokered by the World Bank, is considered one of the most successful water-sharing agreements globally.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
prelimsPoints: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was signed in 1960 between India and Pakistan, brokered by the World Bank.
It allocates the waters of the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India and the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan.
India is allowed to use the waters of Western Rivers for limited non-consumptive uses, including run-of-the-river hydro-power projects.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and significance of the Indus Waters Treaty in India-Pakistan relations.
Discuss the key challenges and points of contention that have emerged under the IWT over the decades.
Evaluate India's strategic options and diplomatic approach in managing water resources disputes with riparian neighbors, particularly Pakistan.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing the Indus River system and the allocated rivers.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty survived three wars between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Initiatives to Boost Tourism Sector and Local Economies
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism unveiled a series of new initiatives aimed at accelerating the revival and growth of India's tourism sector.
• Focus areas include promoting sustainable and responsible tourism, developing niche tourism products (e.g., eco-tourism, adventure tourism, heritage tourism).
• Announced financial incentives for tourism-related infrastructure development and skill enhancement programs for local communities.
• Emphasized digital promotion campaigns and international collaborations to attract a wider range of tourists.
• Aims to leverage tourism for job creation, foreign exchange earnings, and overall socio-economic development.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: The 'Incredible India' campaign is a major initiative by the Ministry of Tourism to promote India as a tourist destination.
Schemes like Swadesh Darshan and PRASHAD focus on thematic development of tourist circuits and pilgrimage rejuvenation.
Tourism is a significant contributor to India's GDP and employment.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-faceted role of the tourism sector in India's economy and its potential for inclusive growth and employment generation.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Indian tourism industry and evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives in addressing them.
Suggest innovative strategies for promoting sustainable and responsible tourism in India, balancing economic benefits with environmental and cultural preservation.
articles.imageDescription: Diverse Indian tourist attractions like Taj Mahal, mountains, and beaches.
articles.trivia: The World Tourism Day is celebrated annually on September 27.

articles.headline: Government Launches Pan-India Scheme to Promote Women Entrepreneurship
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development, in collaboration with MSME Ministry, launched a new national scheme to foster women entrepreneurship.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, mentorship programs, skill development training, and market linkages for women-led businesses.
• Focuses on empowering women across rural and urban areas, promoting self-reliance and economic independence.
• Aims to address gender disparities in entrepreneurship and increase women's participation in the formal economy.
• Includes provisions for easier access to credit and technology for women entrepreneurs.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Social empowerment.
articles.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsPoints: The scheme aims to reduce gender disparity in entrepreneurship.
MSMEs are crucial for job creation and economic growth in India.
Various government schemes like 'Stand-Up India' also support women entrepreneurs.
mainsPoints: Analyze the socio-economic significance of promoting women entrepreneurship in India for achieving inclusive growth and gender equality.
Discuss the key barriers faced by women entrepreneurs in India and evaluate how the new scheme addresses these challenges.
Suggest comprehensive policy measures to create an enabling ecosystem for women-led businesses, encompassing finance, markets, and capacity building.
articles.imageDescription: A group of women discussing business ideas, symbolizing entrepreneurship.
articles.trivia: India ranks 3rd globally in terms of the number of women-owned businesses.

articles.headline: Government Finalizes Policy for Expedited Exploration and Mining of Critical Minerals
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Mines finalized a new policy aimed at expediting the exploration and mining of critical minerals in India.
• The policy introduces enhanced incentives for private sector participation and international collaboration in critical mineral extraction.
• Identifies key critical minerals essential for India's energy transition, defense, and high-tech manufacturing sectors (e.g., Lithium, Cobalt, Rare Earth Elements).
• Streamlines regulatory approvals and promotes sustainable mining practices to minimize environmental impact.
• Aims to reduce India's import dependence and bolster its strategic autonomy in key industrial sectors.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: Critical minerals are essential for economic development and national security, whose supply chains are vulnerable to disruption.
Lithium is crucial for electric vehicle batteries and renewable energy storage.
India recently discovered significant lithium reserves in Jammu & Kashmir.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of critical minerals for India's economic growth, energy transition, and national security.
Discuss the key features of the new policy for critical mineral exploration and mining, evaluating its potential impact on India's self-reliance.
Examine the environmental and geopolitical challenges associated with critical mineral extraction and suggest sustainable solutions.
articles.imageDescription: Various critical minerals like lithium, cobalt, and rare earth elements displayed.
articles.trivia: China currently dominates the global supply chain for many critical minerals, particularly Rare Earth Elements.

articles.headline: India's Ranking in Latest Global Hunger Index Sparks Debate on Food Security Measures
articles.explainer:
• The Global Hunger Index (GHI) 2024 report was released, showing India's improved yet still challenging ranking among surveyed nations.
• The report's methodology and data sources, particularly regarding child wasting and undernourishment, sparked renewed debate.
• Government sources highlighted ongoing efforts through schemes like PM-POSHAN and Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana.
• Critics emphasized the need for more targeted interventions, especially for vulnerable populations and addressing nutritional disparities.
• The discussion underscores the continuous challenge of achieving food security and eradicating malnutrition in India.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to poverty and hunger.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Global Hunger Index / Government of India
prelimsPoints: The Global Hunger Index is a tool that measures and tracks hunger globally, regionally, and nationally.
It is calculated by IFPRI, Concern Worldwide, and Welthungerhilfe.
GHI scores are based on four indicators: undernourishment, child wasting, child stunting, and child mortality.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate India's performance in the Global Hunger Index and discuss the factors contributing to its ranking.
Analyze the effectiveness of various government schemes aimed at ensuring food security and addressing malnutrition in India.
Suggest comprehensive strategies to combat hunger and improve nutritional outcomes, particularly among vulnerable groups, in India.
articles.imageDescription: Children eating a meal, symbolizing food security.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top producers of several agricultural commodities globally.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Discussion Paper on Ethical AI Framework for India
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog released a comprehensive discussion paper on developing an ethical framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) in India.
• The paper addresses critical aspects like data privacy, algorithmic bias, accountability, transparency, and human oversight in AI systems.
• Invites public and stakeholder feedback to shape a balanced policy that fosters AI innovation while mitigating risks.
• Emphasizes a 'human-centric' approach to AI development and deployment, aligning with India's democratic values.
• Aims to position India as a responsible global leader in AI governance and ethical AI practices.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog is the premier policy 'Think Tank' of the Government of India.
Ethical AI frameworks aim to ensure AI systems are fair, transparent, accountable, and safe.
Algorithmic bias occurs when an algorithm produces results that are systematically prejudiced.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical challenges posed by the rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence and the necessity for a robust governance framework.
Analyze the key principles and recommendations of NITI Aayog's discussion paper on ethical AI and its potential impact on India's AI ecosystem.
Evaluate the role of government, industry, and civil society in developing and implementing responsible AI practices in India.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract image of interconnected nodes, representing AI networks.
articles.trivia: Alan Turing is widely considered the father of theoretical computer science and artificial intelligence.

articles.headline: RBI and NABARD Launch Joint Financial Literacy Campaign in Rural India
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and NABARD jointly launched a nationwide financial literacy campaign targeting rural populations.
• The initiative aims to enhance awareness about formal financial products, digital payments, credit discipline, and consumer protection.
• Utilizes local language content, community meetings, and digital platforms to reach diverse demographics.
• Focuses on empowering rural households, farmers, and small entrepreneurs to make informed financial decisions.
• Seeks to promote financial inclusion and reduce reliance on informal credit sources.
gsSubTopics: Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: NABARD (National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development) is an apex development financial institution.
Financial literacy is the ability to understand and effectively use various financial skills.
RBI periodically conducts financial literacy campaigns.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of financial literacy for achieving inclusive growth and empowering rural populations in India.
Analyze the challenges in promoting financial literacy in diverse rural contexts and evaluate the effectiveness of joint initiatives by RBI and NABARD.
Suggest innovative approaches and policy measures to deepen financial inclusion and protect rural consumers from financial frauds.
articles.imageDescription: A rural community meeting discussing financial topics.
articles.trivia: NABARD was established on 12 July 1982 by an Act of Parliament.

articles.headline: Ministry of Culture Launches Initiative to Revive Ancient Indian Textile Heritage
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture launched a nationwide initiative to revive and promote ancient Indian textile traditions and artisanal skills.
• The program focuses on documenting endangered textile techniques, providing training to artisans, and creating market linkages.
• Aims to preserve India's rich cultural heritage, empower traditional craft communities, and promote 'Make in India' in the textile sector.
• Includes collaborations with fashion designers, museums, and academic institutions to showcase traditional textiles globally.
• Emphasizes the sustainable and eco-friendly aspects of traditional Indian textiles.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: India has a rich tradition of textile crafts, including Banarasi silk, Kanchipuram silk, Pashmina, Ikat, etc.
The handloom sector is a significant source of livelihood in rural India.
GI tags help protect the authenticity and uniqueness of traditional products.
mainsPoints: Discuss the cultural and economic significance of India's traditional textile heritage and the threats it faces in the modern era.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives in preserving and promoting traditional crafts and empowering artisan communities.
Suggest innovative strategies to integrate traditional textiles into contemporary markets while ensuring fair compensation for artisans and sustainable practices.
articles.imageDescription: An artisan weaving a traditional Indian textile on a loom.
articles.trivia: India is one of the oldest textile-producing nations in the world, with evidence dating back to the Indus Valley Civilization.

articles.headline: India Joins Global Initiative for Supply Chain Resilience to Mitigate Future Disruptions
articles.explainer:
• India announced its participation in a new multilateral initiative aimed at building resilient global supply chains.
• The initiative, involving several key economies, seeks to diversify supply sources and reduce over-reliance on single regions.
• Focuses on sharing best practices, fostering investment in critical sectors, and developing early warning systems for supply chain disruptions.
• Highlights the lessons learned from recent global crises (e.g., pandemic, geopolitical tensions) on supply chain vulnerabilities.
• Aims to ensure stable and reliable access to essential goods and technologies for participating nations.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests.
prelimsPoints: Supply chain resilience refers to the ability to withstand and recover from disruptions.
The COVID-19 pandemic exposed vulnerabilities in global supply chains.
Diversifying supply sources is a key strategy to enhance resilience.
mainsPoints: Analyze the growing importance of supply chain resilience in the contemporary global economy and India's strategic interest in such initiatives.
Discuss the challenges in building resilient global supply chains and the role of international cooperation in addressing them.
Evaluate the potential benefits and implications of India's participation in multilateral supply chain resilience initiatives for its economic security and trade relations.
articles.imageDescription: Interconnected global map with arrows indicating trade routes, symbolizing supply chains.
articles.trivia: The 'just-in-time' (JIT) inventory management system, while efficient, was found vulnerable during major supply chain shocks.

highlights: India advocates for equitable global climate finance reforms at the UN General Assembly, emphasizing MDB restructuring and developed nations' commitments.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5% amidst easing inflation and robust growth projections, focusing on financial stability.
Supreme Court directs Centre to mediate an inter-state water dispute, upholding principles of cooperative federalism.
ISRO outlines the roadmap for its Chandrayaan-4 mission, with a primary objective of achieving a lunar sample return.
Parliamentary panel recommends key amendments to the Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, strengthening accountability and user consent.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India, consider the following statements:
1. It consists of seven members: four from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.
2. The Governor of the Reserve Bank of India is the ex-officio Chairperson of the MPC.
3. The primary objective of the MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The MPC consists of six members: three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government. Statement 2 is correct. The RBI Governor is the ex-officio Chairperson. Statement 3 is correct. Price stability with growth is the primary objective.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is India's National Aquatic Animal?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Ganges River Dolphin (Platanista gangetica) was declared India's National Aquatic Animal in 2009. It is listed as 'Endangered' on the IUCN Red List.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following components of the 'India Stack':
1. Aadhaar
2. Unified Payments Interface (UPI)
3. DigiLocker
4. Goods and Services Tax Network (GSTN)
Which of the components given above are part of the 'India Stack' as a Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'India Stack' primarily refers to the set of open APIs and digital public goods that aim to unlock the economic primitives of identity, data, and payments at population scale. Key components are Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, along with the consent layer architecture. GSTN is a separate digital infrastructure for tax administration.

prelimsMocks.question:
With respect to the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT), consider the following statements:
1. It was signed between India and Pakistan in 1960, with the World Bank as a guarantor.
2. The Treaty allocates the waters of the Eastern Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to India and the Western Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to Pakistan.
3. India is allowed to use the waters of the Western Rivers for limited non-consumptive uses, including run-of-the-river hydro-power projects.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The IWT was signed in 1960. Statement 2 is incorrect. The Treaty allocates the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India and the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan. Statement 3 is correct. India has limited non-consumptive use rights on Western Rivers.

prelimsMocks.question: Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) in India is mandated under which of the following acts?
options: (b) Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974
(d) Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process in India is statutorily backed by the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986. The first EIA notification was issued in 1994 under this Act.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze India's evolving stance on global climate finance. What reforms are needed in multilateral development banks to ensure equitable and accessible climate finance for developing nations? (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests; Important International institutions, agencies and fora – their structure, mandate. GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's position at UNGA on climate finance, emphasizing CBDR and historical responsibility. Discuss the inadequacy and inaccessibility of current climate finance. Propose reforms for MDBs (e.g., capital infusion, innovative financing, simpler access, integration of climate risks). Conclude with India's leadership role.

mainsMocks.question: The Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023, seeks to establish a robust framework for data privacy in India. Discuss the key provisions of the Bill and the significance of the recent parliamentary committee recommendations in addressing concerns related to data governance and individual rights. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies. GS3: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the DPDP Bill's aim and context (Puttaswamy judgment). Explain key provisions like data fiduciary, data principal rights, consent, cross-border data transfer, and Data Protection Board. Detail the parliamentary committee's recommendations (e.g., stronger accountability, clearer government access rules, independent board). Conclude on the balance between privacy, innovation, and national interest.

mainsMocks.question: Urban flooding has become a recurrent challenge for Indian cities, exacerbated by climate change and unplanned development. Examine the major causes of urban flooding and evaluate the effectiveness of current management strategies. Suggest a comprehensive, multi-pronged approach to build climate-resilient urban infrastructure. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Important Geophysical phenomena; Urbanization, their problems and their remedies. GS3: Disaster and disaster management.
mainsMocks.approach: Define urban flooding and its causes (e.g., intense rainfall, inadequate drainage, concretization, encroachment, climate change). Critically evaluate existing strategies (e.g., reactive measures, piecemeal infrastructure). Propose a multi-pronged approach: early warning systems, integrated stormwater management, nature-based solutions, stringent urban planning, community participation, and inter-agency coordination. Conclude with the need for a holistic, proactive approach.

quickBytes.text: World Ozone Day is observed annually on September 16, commemorating the signing of the Montreal Protocol.
The Bhitarkanika National Park in Odisha is known for its mangrove ecosystem and saltwater crocodiles.
The 'Sagarmala Programme' is a flagship initiative of the Government of India for port-led development.
Article 352 of the Indian Constitution deals with the Proclamation of National Emergency.
The term 'Blue Economy' refers to the sustainable use of ocean resources for economic growth.
The 'Operation Ganga' was launched by India to evacuate its citizens from Ukraine in 2022.
The 'Panchayati Raj Day' is celebrated on April 24 every year in India.
The 'White Revolution' in India is associated with the increase in milk production.
The 'Great Indian Bustard' is a critically endangered bird found in grasslands of India and Pakistan.
The 'Digital India' programme was launched in 2015 to transform India into a digitally empowered society.

GS1.headline: Urban Local Bodies Adopt New Strategy for Enhanced Urban Flood Management
GS1.explainer:
• Several major urban local bodies across India announced the adoption of a new comprehensive strategy for urban flood management.
• The strategy integrates early warning systems, improved drainage infrastructure, nature-based solutions, and community preparedness programs.
• Focuses on leveraging technology like AI and IoT for real-time monitoring of weather patterns and water levels.
• Aims to reduce urban vulnerability to extreme rainfall events and climate change impacts.
• Calls for inter-agency coordination and citizen participation for effective implementation.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including waterbodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
GS1.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs / NDMA
prelimsPoints: Urban flooding is caused by intense rainfall that overwhelms the capacity of drainage systems.
Nature-based solutions include green infrastructure like permeable pavements, rain gardens, and wetlands.
The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) issues guidelines for urban flood management.
mainsPoints: Analyze the major causes and consequences of urban flooding in India, linking it to climate change and unplanned urbanization.
Discuss the effectiveness of current urban flood management strategies and propose a holistic approach incorporating technology and nature-based solutions.
Examine the role of urban local bodies, state governments, and citizens in building climate-resilient cities against flood hazards.
GS1.imageDescription: Flooded urban street with cars partially submerged.
GS1.trivia: Mumbai's Mithi River, once a major natural drain, has been severely encroached upon, exacerbating urban flooding in the city.

GS1.headline: Government Launches Pan-India Scheme to Promote Women Entrepreneurship
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development, in collaboration with MSME Ministry, launched a new national scheme to foster women entrepreneurship.
• The scheme provides financial assistance, mentorship programs, skill development training, and market linkages for women-led businesses.
• Focuses on empowering women across rural and urban areas, promoting self-reliance and economic independence.
• Aims to address gender disparities in entrepreneurship and increase women's participation in the formal economy.
• Includes provisions for easier access to credit and technology for women entrepreneurs.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Social empowerment.
GS1.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsPoints: The scheme aims to reduce gender disparity in entrepreneurship.
MSMEs are crucial for job creation and economic growth in India.
Various government schemes like 'Stand-Up India' also support women entrepreneurs.
mainsPoints: Analyze the socio-economic significance of promoting women entrepreneurship in India for achieving inclusive growth and gender equality.
Discuss the key barriers faced by women entrepreneurs in India and evaluate how the new scheme addresses these challenges.
Suggest comprehensive policy measures to create an enabling ecosystem for women-led businesses, encompassing finance, markets, and capacity building.
GS1.imageDescription: A group of women discussing business ideas, symbolizing entrepreneurship.
GS1.trivia: India ranks 3rd globally in terms of the number of women-owned businesses.

GS1.headline: Ministry of Culture Launches Initiative to Revive Ancient Indian Textile Heritage
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture launched a nationwide initiative to revive and promote ancient Indian textile traditions and artisanal skills.
• The program focuses on documenting endangered textile techniques, providing training to artisans, and creating market linkages.
• Aims to preserve India's rich cultural heritage, empower traditional craft communities, and promote 'Make in India' in the textile sector.
• Includes collaborations with fashion designers, museums, and academic institutions to showcase traditional textiles globally.
• Emphasizes the sustainable and eco-friendly aspects of traditional Indian textiles.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: India has a rich tradition of textile crafts, including Banarasi silk, Kanchipuram silk, Pashmina, Ikat, etc.
The handloom sector is a significant source of livelihood in rural India.
GI tags help protect the authenticity and uniqueness of traditional products.
mainsPoints: Discuss the cultural and economic significance of India's traditional textile heritage and the threats it faces in the modern era.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government initiatives in preserving and promoting traditional crafts and empowering artisan communities.
Suggest innovative strategies to integrate traditional textiles into contemporary markets while ensuring fair compensation for artisans and sustainable practices.
GS1.imageDescription: An artisan weaving a traditional Indian textile on a loom.
GS1.trivia: India is one of the oldest textile-producing nations in the world, with evidence dating back to the Indus Valley Civilization.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Equitable Global Climate Finance Reforms at UNGA
GS2.explainer:
• India's External Affairs Minister addressed the UN General Assembly, emphasizing the need for equitable and accessible climate finance for developing nations.
• Highlighted the disproportionate burden faced by developing countries in combating climate change despite lower historical emissions.
• Proposed reforms to multilateral development banks (MDBs) to enhance their capacity for climate financing.
• Stressed the importance of technology transfer and capacity building alongside financial support.
• Called for developed nations to fulfill their $100 billion annual climate finance commitment.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora – their structure, mandate.
GS2.source: Ministry of External Affairs / UN News
prelimsPoints: UN General Assembly (UNGA) is the main deliberative, policymaking and representative organ of the UN.
The $100 billion climate finance commitment was made by developed countries under the UNFCCC framework.
Multilateral Development Banks (MDBs) include institutions like the World Bank, IMF, ADB, AfDB, EBRD, IDB.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze India's stand on climate finance and its implications for global climate action.
Discuss the challenges faced by developing countries in accessing climate finance and propose solutions.
Examine the role of MDBs in addressing climate change and suggest reforms for enhanced effectiveness.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian delegation addressing the UN General Assembly podium.
GS2.trivia: The UN General Assembly convenes annually in regular session from September to December, and thereafter as required.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Directs Centre to Mediate Inter-State Water Dispute, Upholds Federal Spirit
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a significant ruling, directed the Union government to actively mediate in a long-standing inter-state water dispute between two major states.
• Emphasized that while judicial intervention is necessary, resolution through cooperative federalism is paramount for such disputes.
• Highlighted the constitutional provisions empowering the Centre to facilitate amicable settlements in water-sharing conflicts.
• Urged states to engage in constructive dialogue, reminding them of their shared responsibility towards national development.
• The ruling aims to prevent prolonged litigation and promote collaborative governance in resource management.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: Article 262 of the Indian Constitution deals with adjudication of disputes relating to waters of inter-state rivers or river valleys.
The Parliament has enacted the Inter-State Water Disputes Act, 1956, and the River Boards Act, 1956.
Inter-State Water Disputes Tribunals are established under the 1956 Act.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of the Supreme Court and the Union government in resolving inter-state water disputes, citing relevant constitutional provisions.
Discuss the concept of cooperative federalism in the context of resource sharing in India and its challenges.
Suggest reforms to the existing dispute resolution mechanisms for inter-state water conflicts to ensure timely and equitable solutions.
GS2.trivia: The first Inter-State Water Disputes Tribunal was established in 1969 to resolve the Krishna water dispute.

GS2.headline: India and Maldives Conclude Joint Naval Exercise 'Ekatha' Enhancing Maritime Security
GS2.explainer:
• India and Maldives successfully concluded the 17th edition of their joint naval exercise, 'Ekatha', in the Indian Ocean region.
• The exercise focused on enhancing interoperability, maritime domain awareness, and HADR (Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief) operations.
• Participating units practiced anti-piracy drills, search and rescue operations, and communication procedures.
• The exercise underscores the commitment of both nations to regional security and stability in the strategically important Indian Ocean.
• High-level observers from both navies emphasized the importance of such engagements for mutual security interests.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
keyTerms: SAGAR (Security And Growth for All in the Region)
prelimsPoints: Exercise 'Ekatha' is a regular joint naval exercise between India and Maldives.
Maldives is a key maritime neighbour of India in the Indian Ocean.
India's 'SAGAR' doctrine emphasizes security and growth for all in the Indian Ocean Region.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of India-Maldives defense cooperation in the context of India's broader maritime security strategy.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for India in strengthening its security partnerships with Indian Ocean littoral states.
Evaluate the role of joint military exercises in enhancing regional stability and addressing common security threats.
GS2.imageDescription: Naval ships of India and Maldives participating in a joint exercise.
GS2.trivia: Maldives is the smallest Asian country in both land area and population.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Amendments to Digital Personal Data Protection Bill
GS2.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) submitted its report on the Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023, recommending several key amendments.
• The recommendations focus on strengthening data fiduciary accountability, enhancing user consent mechanisms, and clarifying data localization requirements.
• Suggested a more robust framework for cross-border data transfers and stricter penalties for data breaches.
• Addressed concerns regarding government access to personal data and the independence of the Data Protection Board.
• The report paves the way for further debate and potential modifications before the Bill is presented for parliamentary approval.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
GS2.source: Parliament of India / Lok Sabha Secretariat
prelimsPoints: The Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023, aims to provide for the processing of digital personal data in a manner that recognizes both the right of individuals to protect their personal data and the need to process such data for lawful purposes.
The Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21.
Data Protection Board of India is proposed to be established under the Bill.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Bill, 2023, and the amendments proposed by the Parliamentary Committee.
Discuss the challenges in balancing individual privacy rights with national security and economic development imperatives in the digital age.
Evaluate the potential impact of the Bill on India's digital economy, technological innovation, and international data flows.
GS2.imageDescription: Abstract representation of data flow and security.
GS2.trivia: The first attempt at a comprehensive data protection law in India was the Srikrishna Committee Report in 2018.

GS2.headline: India-Brazil Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Expanding Bilateral Trade and Investment
GS2.explainer:
• India and Brazil held their annual Strategic Dialogue, focusing on boosting bilateral trade and investment across various sectors.
• Discussions included cooperation in agriculture, renewable energy, defense manufacturing, and pharmaceuticals.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to strengthening BRICS and IBSA frameworks for South-South cooperation.
• Explored avenues for reducing trade barriers and diversifying their trade basket to achieve a target of $20 billion by 2030.
• Emphasized shared values of democracy and multilateralism in addressing global challenges.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests.
keyTerms: BRICS (Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa)
IBSA (India, Brazil, South Africa Dialogue Forum)
prelimsPoints: Brazil is India's largest trading partner in the Latin American region.
BRICS is an association of five major emerging national economies.
IBSA Dialogue Forum is a trilateral developmental initiative.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-Brazil strategic partnership for both nations and the broader South-South cooperation agenda.
Discuss the potential for enhancing bilateral trade and investment between India and Brazil, identifying key sectors and challenges.
Evaluate the role of groupings like BRICS and IBSA in promoting multilateralism and addressing global economic imbalances.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Brazil together.
GS2.trivia: Brazil is the only Portuguese-speaking country in South America.

GS2.headline: Review Meeting Highlights Progress and Challenges in National Education Policy Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education held a high-level review meeting to assess the progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation.
• Key achievements highlighted include curriculum reforms, increased focus on vocational education, and digital learning initiatives.
• Challenges identified pertain to infrastructure development, teacher training, and equitable access in remote areas.
• States were urged to accelerate reforms, particularly in early childhood care and education (ECCE) and higher education accreditation.
• Emphasized the importance of continuous monitoring and adaptive strategies for successful NEP rollout.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
It aims to transform India's education system to meet the needs of the 21st century.
Key pillars include access, equity, quality, affordability, and accountability.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of NEP 2020 in reforming India's education system and fostering human resource development.
Discuss the major challenges in the implementation of NEP 2020, particularly in terms of infrastructure, teacher training, and digital divide.
Suggest concrete measures to ensure equitable and effective rollout of NEP across all states and educational levels.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a classroom with modern learning tools.
GS2.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was instrumental in shaping India's education policy, recommending a common education structure.

GS2.headline: India and Japan Hold Strategic Energy Dialogue, Focus on Green Hydrogen and Renewables
GS2.explainer:
• India and Japan conducted their latest Strategic Energy Dialogue, reaffirming commitment to clean energy transition and energy security.
• Discussions centered on accelerating cooperation in green hydrogen production, renewable energy technologies, and energy efficiency.
• Explored joint ventures in critical mineral supply chains necessary for battery manufacturing and EV production.
• Emphasized the importance of diversifying energy sources and reducing reliance on fossil fuels for climate goals.
• Both nations aim to strengthen collaboration on smart grids and energy storage solutions.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests.
prelimsPoints: Japan is a major investor in India's infrastructure and energy sectors.
Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
India aims for 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of India-Japan energy cooperation in achieving energy security and climate goals for both nations.
Discuss the potential of green hydrogen and renewable energy technologies in transforming India's energy landscape.
Examine the challenges in developing a robust critical mineral supply chain and the role of international partnerships like India-Japan.
GS2.imageDescription: Solar panels and a wind turbine against a blue sky, symbolizing renewable energy.
GS2.trivia: Japan's bullet train technology has been adopted for India's Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail project.

GS2.headline: Report Highlights Progress and Gaps in India's Health Sector Digitalization
GS2.explainer:
• A recent report by a leading think tank assessed the progress of digitalization in India's health sector, particularly under the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM).
• Noted significant advancements in creating digital health IDs, electronic health records (EHRs), and teleconsultation services.
• Identified gaps including uneven adoption across states, infrastructure deficits in rural areas, and challenges in data interoperability and privacy.
• Recommended enhanced investment in digital health infrastructure, skilled workforce development, and robust data governance frameworks.
• Stressed the potential of digital health to improve accessibility, affordability, and quality of healthcare services.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) aims to develop the backbone necessary to support the integrated digital health infrastructure of the country.
It focuses on creating a seamless online platform through the provision of a wide range of data, information and infrastructure services.
The National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for ABDM.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the transformative potential of digitalization in improving healthcare access, efficiency, and quality in India.
Discuss the major challenges in implementing the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) and achieving universal digital health coverage.
Suggest policy measures to address issues of digital divide, data privacy, and interoperability in India's health sector digitalization efforts.
GS2.imageDescription: Doctor using a tablet for patient consultation, symbolizing digital health.
GS2.trivia: India's first National Health Policy was formulated in 1983.

GS2.headline: India Reaffirms Commitment to Indus Waters Treaty Amidst Ongoing Discussions
GS2.explainer:
• India's Ministry of External Affairs reaffirmed its commitment to the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) during ongoing technical discussions with Pakistan.
• The discussions, held under the Permanent Indus Commission (PIC) framework, addressed issues related to water sharing and project construction on western rivers.
• Emphasized India's desire for amicable resolution of differences through established mechanisms of the Treaty.
• Highlighted India's rights to utilize the waters of the western rivers for non-consumptive uses as per the IWT.
• The Treaty, brokered by the World Bank, is considered one of the most successful water-sharing agreements globally.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
prelimsPoints: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was signed in 1960 between India and Pakistan, brokered by the World Bank.
It allocates the waters of the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India and the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan.
India is allowed to use the waters of Western Rivers for limited non-consumptive uses, including run-of-the-river hydro-power projects.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and significance of the Indus Waters Treaty in India-Pakistan relations.
Discuss the key challenges and points of contention that have emerged under the IWT over the decades.
Evaluate India's strategic options and diplomatic approach in managing water resources disputes with riparian neighbors, particularly Pakistan.
GS2.imageDescription: Map showing the Indus River system and the allocated rivers.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty survived three wars between India and Pakistan.

GS2.headline: India's Ranking in Latest Global Hunger Index Sparks Debate on Food Security Measures
GS2.explainer:
• The Global Hunger Index (GHI) 2024 report was released, showing India's improved yet still challenging ranking among surveyed nations.
• The report's methodology and data sources, particularly regarding child wasting and undernourishment, sparked renewed debate.
• Government sources highlighted ongoing efforts through schemes like PM-POSHAN and Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Anna Yojana.
• Critics emphasized the need for more targeted interventions, especially for vulnerable populations and addressing nutritional disparities.
• The discussion underscores the continuous challenge of achieving food security and eradicating malnutrition in India.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to poverty and hunger.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
GS2.source: Global Hunger Index / Government of India
prelimsPoints: The Global Hunger Index is a tool that measures and tracks hunger globally, regionally, and nationally.
It is calculated by IFPRI, Concern Worldwide, and Welthungerhilfe.
GHI scores are based on four indicators: undernourishment, child wasting, child stunting, and child mortality.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate India's performance in the Global Hunger Index and discuss the factors contributing to its ranking.
Analyze the effectiveness of various government schemes aimed at ensuring food security and addressing malnutrition in India.
Suggest comprehensive strategies to combat hunger and improve nutritional outcomes, particularly among vulnerable groups, in India.
GS2.imageDescription: Children eating a meal, symbolizing food security.
GS2.trivia: India ranks among the top producers of several agricultural commodities globally.

GS2.headline: India Joins Global Initiative for Supply Chain Resilience to Mitigate Future Disruptions
GS2.explainer:
• India announced its participation in a new multilateral initiative aimed at building resilient global supply chains.
• The initiative, involving several key economies, seeks to diversify supply sources and reduce over-reliance on single regions.
• Focuses on sharing best practices, fostering investment in critical sectors, and developing early warning systems for supply chain disruptions.
• Highlights the lessons learned from recent global crises (e.g., pandemic, geopolitical tensions) on supply chain vulnerabilities.
• Aims to ensure stable and reliable access to essential goods and technologies for participating nations.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India's interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests.
prelimsPoints: Supply chain resilience refers to the ability to withstand and recover from disruptions.
The COVID-19 pandemic exposed vulnerabilities in global supply chains.
Diversifying supply sources is a key strategy to enhance resilience.
mainsPoints: Analyze the growing importance of supply chain resilience in the contemporary global economy and India's strategic interest in such initiatives.
Discuss the challenges in building resilient global supply chains and the role of international cooperation in addressing them.
Evaluate the potential benefits and implications of India's participation in multilateral supply chain resilience initiatives for its economic security and trade relations.
GS2.imageDescription: Interconnected global map with arrows indicating trade routes, symbolizing supply chains.
GS2.trivia: The 'just-in-time' (JIT) inventory management system, while efficient, was found vulnerable during major supply chain shocks.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflationary Pressures, Focus on Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its latest review, keeping the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• Citing signs of easing retail inflation and robust economic growth projections, the MPC adopted a cautious 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
• Emphasis was placed on monitoring global commodity prices and geopolitical developments that could impact domestic inflation.
• Governor highlighted the resilience of the Indian economy and the need to support sustainable growth while managing price stability.
• Announced measures to enhance liquidity management and strengthen financial sector stability.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) consists of six members: three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.
The primary objective of monetary policy in India is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks in the event of any shortfall of funds.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its likely impact on the Indian economy.
Discuss the challenges faced by the MPC in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global scenario.
Evaluate the effectiveness of RBI's monetary policy tools in achieving its stated objectives.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India headquarters building.
GS3.trivia: The first Governor of the Reserve Bank of India was Sir Osborne Smith, while the first Indian Governor was C.D. Deshmukh.
